rationale: The citing paper uses Dhar and Wertenbroch (2000) in Study 3 hypothesis development to justify a core theoretical claim: utilitarian purchases are more cognitively driven than hedonic purchases. That claim is then used to predict and test when attribution-based moderation (donation amount x company-cause fit) should appear, making the citation functionally important to model setup and interpretation. The appendix also lists Dhar and Wertenbroch as a source for the hedonism/utilitarianism operationalization, indicating design-level use rather than a passing background mention.
relation_description: The citing paper applies the hedonic-versus-utilitarian framework from Dhar and Wertenbroch to a new cause-related marketing context. It uses that framework to derive and test moderation predictions about willingness to pay under different donation and fit conditions.
